approach: Mirror handleLifecycleAction pattern — call deps.updateContent in handleApprove/handleRegenerate after context writes, before terminal dispatch. Harden STATUS_LIFECYCLE preamble with a prescriptive MUST-DO-BEFORE-ENDING checklist that forces the AI to set completedAt, flip status to completed form, and print Done {step}.
last_action: PR #117 opened — feat(spec-viewer): repaint viewer on approve/regenerate and force AI completion marker.

specify.key_finding: handleLifecycleAction at messageHandlers.ts:312 already calls deps.updateContent after setStatus/reactivate — handleApprove/handleRegenerate can reuse that exact pattern.

tasks.key_finding: Plan restricts scope to two files (messageHandlers.ts, promptBuilder.ts); T001+T002 reuse handleLifecycleAction's updateContent pattern, T003 hardens STATUS_LIFECYCLE preamble.

implement.key_finding: Clean compile, clean commit on feat/immediate-status-update. Version bump from /install-local (0.12.0 → 0.12.1) was un-staged by pre:commit hook and kept out of the PR per project convention.

T001.did: Added deps.updateContent() in handleApprove in both navigable-next and actionOnly-implement branches, after startStep and before executeStepInTerminal.

T002.did: Added deps.updateContent() in handleRegenerate after startStep and before executeStepInTerminal.

T003.did: Rewrote renderPreamble to emit a MUST-DO-BEFORE-ENDING block with three required actions — set completedAt, flip status to the per-step completed form, print the per-step Done phrase. Added COMPLETED_STATUS_BY_STEP and DONE_PHRASE_BY_STEP maps keyed by PromptStep.
concerns: PromptStep is narrower than StepName — it only covers specify/plan/tasks/implement (the keys of CANONICAL_SUBSTEPS). 'clarify' and 'analyze' lifecycle steps go through the untouched command path (isKnownStep returns false) and fall back to the raw command with no preamble. This matches pre-existing behavior, but means the new hard requirement does not apply to those two steps.

decisions: Added per-step completed-status and Done-phrase maps in promptBuilder.ts rather than parsing STATUS_LIFECYCLE at runtime — keeps the prescriptive block per-step concrete (e.g., 'flip status to "planned"' instead of 'flip status to the completed form') so the AI cannot misread the rule.

concerns.note: Preamble hard requirement only fires for PromptStep keys (specify/plan/tasks/implement). Lifecycle steps 'clarify' and 'analyze' bypass the preamble entirely because isKnownStep returns false — pre-existing behavior, out of scope for this spec but worth flagging for a future pass.
